asymmetry: Landlords are legally evicting families with zero wrongdoing, deliberately racing to beat a ban that would have protected those same families.
hook: Your landlord can legally kick you out tomorrow.
core_fact: Section 21 'no fault' evictions allow landlords to remove tenants who have done absolutely nothing wrong — and landlords are filing them en masse right now specifically to beat the Renters Rights Bill ban, leaving families with as little as 2 months to find a new home in a market where average UK rents hit a record £1,367/month in 2024.
villain: Private landlords exploiting the Section 21 loophole before Parliament closes it
share_trigger: Did you see this? Your landlord can legally throw your family out for no reason and loads of them are doing it right now before the law changes.
search_query: Section 21 no fault eviction UK families 2024 Renters Rights Bill
